content:
# Numbers Chapter 1 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios ~pp. 145–147; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 110–111 (LXX) | **Source Image:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/NUM_001_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_001_page_002.jpg`, `NUM_001_page_003.jpg`

---

**1** And YHWH spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, in the tent of meeting, on the first day of the second month, in the second year of their going out from the land of Egypt, saying,

**2** "Take up the sum of all the congregation of the sons of Israel, by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, every male by their heads,

**3** from twenty years old and upward, all who go out to the host in Israel -- you shall number them by their hosts, you and Aaron.

**4** And with you there shall be one man from each tribe, each one the head of the house of his fathers.

**5** And these are the names of the men who shall stand with you: for Reuben, Elizur son of Shedeur;

**6** for Simeon, Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai;

**7** for Judah, Nahshon son of Amminadab;

**8** for Issachar, Nethanel son of Zuar;

**9** for Zebulun, Eliab son of Helon;

**10** for the sons of Joseph: for Ephraim, Elishama son of Ammihud; for Manasseh, Gamaliel son of Pedahzur;

**11** for Benjamin, Abidan son of Gideoni;

**12** for Dan, Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai;

**13** for Asher, Pagiel son of Ochran;

**14** for Gad, Eliasaph son of Deuel;

**15** for Naphtali, Ahira son of Enan."

**16** These were the called ones of the congregation, the leaders of the tribes of their fathers; they were the heads of the thousands of Israel.

**17** And Moses and Aaron took these men who had been designated by name.

**18** And they assembled all the congregation on the first day of the second month, and they registered themselves by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, by their heads,

**19** as YHWH had commanded Moses. And he numbered them in the wilderness of Sinai.

**20** And the sons of Reuben, the firstborn of Israel, their generations by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, by their heads, every male from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host --

**21** their numbered ones of the tribe of Reuben: forty-six thousand and five hundred.

**22** Of the sons of Simeon, their generations by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, his numbered ones by the number of names, by their heads, every male from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host --

**23** their numbered ones of the tribe of Simeon: fifty-nine thousand and three hundred.

**24** Of the sons of Gad, their generations by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host --

**25** their numbered ones of the tribe of Gad: forty-five thousand and six hundred and fifty.

**26** Of the sons of Judah, their generations by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host --

**27** their numbered ones of the tribe of Judah: seventy-four thousand and six hundred.

**28** Of the sons of Issachar, their generations by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host --

**29** their numbered ones of the tribe of Issachar: fifty-four thousand and four hundred.

**30** Of the sons of Zebulun, their generations by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host --

**31** their numbered ones of the tribe of Zebulun: fifty-seven thousand and four hundred.

**32** Of the sons of Joseph: of the sons of Ephraim, their generations by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host --

**33** their numbered ones of the tribe of Ephraim: forty thousand and five hundred.

**34** Of the sons of Manasseh, their generations by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host --

**35** their numbered ones of the tribe of Manasseh: thirty-two thousand and two hundred.

**36** Of the sons of Benjamin, their generations by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host --

**37** their numbered ones of the tribe of Benjamin: thirty-five thousand and four hundred.

**38** Of the sons of Dan, their generations by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host --

**39** their numbered ones of the tribe of Dan: sixty-two thousand and seven hundred.

**40** Of the sons of Asher, their generations by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host --

**41** their numbered ones of the tribe of Asher: forty-one thousand and five hundred.

**42** Of the sons of Naphtali, their generations by their clans, by the houses of their fathers, by the number of names, from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host --

**43** their numbered ones of the tribe of Naphtali: fifty-three thousand and four hundred.

**44** These are the ones who were numbered, whom Moses and Aaron numbered, and the leaders of Israel, twelve men, each one for the house of his fathers.

**45** And all the numbered ones of the sons of Israel, by the houses of their fathers, from twenty years old and upward, all who went out to the host in Israel --

**46** and all the numbered ones were six hundred and three thousand and five hundred and fifty.

**47** But the Levites, by the tribe of their fathers, were not numbered among them.

**48** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

**49** "Only the tribe of Levi you shall not number, and their sum you shall not take up among the sons of Israel.

**50** But you, appoint the Levites over the tabernacle of the testimony, and over all its vessels, and over all that belongs to it. They shall carry the tabernacle and all its vessels, and they shall minister to it, and they shall encamp around the tabernacle.

**51** And when the tabernacle sets out, the Levites shall take it down; and when the tabernacle encamps, the Levites shall set it up. And the stranger who comes near shall be put to death.

**52** And the sons of Israel shall encamp, each man by his camp, and each man by his standard, according to their hosts.

**53** But the Levites shall encamp around the tabernacle of the testimony, so that there shall be no wrath upon the congregation of the sons of Israel. And the Levites shall keep the charge of the tabernacle of the testimony."

**54** And the sons of Israel did according to all that YHWH had commanded Moses; so they did.

---

## LOGOS Translation Notes

### Manuscript Observations
The Leningrad Codex (B19A) pages 145--147 present Numbers 1 in its entirety, beginning with the parashah opening vaydabber YHWH el-Mosheh (p. 145, right column). The text is written in the standard three-column format with Masorah parva notations visible in the margins. The tribal census formulae (vv. 20--43) follow the characteristic repetitive pattern with only the tribe name and census figure varying. Page 146 continues through the census lists, and page 147 concludes the chapter with the Levitical exemption (vv. 47--54) and transitions toward Numbers 2. The Codex Vaticanus (pp. 110--111) was consulted for cross-reference; the Greek Arithmoi text follows the same structural pattern, with the LXX preserving the highly formulaic census language in close correspondence to the MT.

### Key Translation Decisions

1. **"Take up the sum" (v. 2):** Hebrew se'u et-rosh translates literally as "lift up the head," an idiom for conducting a census or reckoning. The rendering "take up the sum" preserves the formal action of the Hebrew verb nasa' while conveying the census function. The LXX renders labete archen, "take the beginning/sum," similarly interpreting the idiom.

2. **"Host" (vv. 3, 20, etc.):** Hebrew tsava' is rendered consistently as "host" throughout the chapter rather than "army" or "warfare." The term carries military connotations (those mustered for service) but its semantic range encompasses organized assembly under divine command. "Host" preserves this breadth.

3. **"Clans" (vv. 2, 18, 20, etc.):** Hebrew mishpechot is rendered "clans" to distinguish it from the larger unit "tribe" (matteh/shevet) and the smaller unit "house of the father" (beyt 'av). This three-tiered kinship structure -- tribe, clan, father's house -- is maintained throughout the census formulae.

4. **"Called ones of the congregation" (v. 16):** Hebrew qeru'ey ha'edah preserves the passive participle -- these men are "called" or "summoned" rather than self-appointed. The further designation "heads of the thousands of Israel" (roshey 'alfey Yisra'el) identifies them as military-administrative leaders over units of a thousand.

5. **"Registered themselves" (v. 18):** Hebrew vayyityaledu, a Hithpael form from the root yalad ("to beget"), means literally "they declared their births/lineage." The reflexive stem indicates self-registration by lineage declaration. "Registered themselves" captures the reflexive action while indicating the genealogical basis of the process.

6. **"Deuel" (v. 14):** The MT reads Deuel (דעואל) here, while in Numbers 2:14 the same figure appears as Reuel (רעואל). The dalet/resh confusion is a well-known scribal variation in Hebrew manuscripts (the two letters are graphically similar). This translation follows the Leningrad Codex reading at each occurrence. The LXX reads Ragouēl in both passages, suggesting the Vorlage may have read Reuel consistently.

7. **"Tabernacle of the testimony" (vv. 50, 53):** Hebrew mishkan ha'edut is rendered literally. The "testimony" ('edut) refers to the covenant tablets housed within the ark. The LXX renders skēnē tou martyriou ("tent of the witness/testimony"), confirming the same referent.

8. **"The stranger who comes near shall be put to death" (v. 51):** Hebrew veha-zar ha-qarev yumat. The term zar here does not mean "foreigner" in an ethnic sense but "outsider" -- anyone not of Levitical descent who encroaches upon the sacred precincts. "Stranger" is retained as the formal equivalent, with the death penalty formula (yumat, Hophal of mut) rendered straightforwardly.

9. **"Wrath" (v. 53):** Hebrew qetseph refers to divine wrath or fury. The Levitical encampment around the tabernacle serves as a protective cordon so that qetseph does not fall upon the congregation. The term is rendered literally rather than softened to "anger" or "displeasure."

10. **Census figures:** The twelve tribal totals (vv. 21--43) sum to 603,550 (v. 46), consistent with the MT and with Exodus 38:26. All numbers follow the Hebrew text of the Leningrad Codex exactly. The LXX figures correspond in all cases.

### Consistent Term Renderings (Numbers Series)
| Hebrew | LOGOS Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| bney Yisra'el | sons of Israel | Not "children of Israel" or "Israelites" |
| YHWH | YHWH | Transliterated; not "the LORD" |
| mishkan ha'edut | tabernacle of the testimony | Literal; not "tabernacle of witness" |
| tsava' | host | Military muster; not "army" |
| matteh | tribe | Standard administrative unit |
| mishpachah | clan | Kinship unit between tribe and father's house |
| beyt 'av | house of (his/their) fathers | Patrilineal household |
| nasa' rosh | take up the sum | Census idiom; lit. "lift the head" |
| zar | stranger | Non-Levite in sacral context |
| qetseph | wrath | Divine wrath specifically |

--- LOGOS
